general_notes: Long-term follow-up data from Phase 3 immunotherapy trials. Each row specifies a trial and a landmark OS time point. Values must come from the peer-reviewed publication that reports overall survival at that specific time point — this may or may not be the primary trial publication. The absolute OS benefit is the difference between the two randomized arms at the landmark. Cite the source paper used.

search_groups.description: Trial name with experimental drug, indication, and OS landmark time point.

Overall survival rates and absolute benefit at the specified landmark time point. Requires identifying the correct published source for each trial and time point, extracting OS rates for both arms, and computing the absolute difference. Each trial has its own nuance regarding arm definitions, published updates, and patient population.

validation_targets.description: Trial name with investigational drug, indication, and OS landmark time point.
examples: PACIFIC (durvalumab / unresectable stage III NSCLC post-CRT) — 4-yr OS

validation_targets.description: Overall survival rate (%) at the specified landmark time point for the experimental arm. Report to 1 decimal place.
validation_targets.notes: Source the value from the publication reporting OS at the queried time point. Use the intention-to-treat or all-randomized population unless otherwise specified. The experimental arm is the investigational drug arm named in the Trial column.

validation_targets.description: Absolute difference in OS at the landmark between the two main randomized arms: experimental arm OS% minus control arm OS%, in percentage points. Report to 1 decimal place.
validation_targets.notes: Compute as (experimental arm OS%) − (control arm OS%) from the same publication and same ITT population. A positive value means experimental arm had higher OS. This value is typically not directly stated — it must be derived from the two arm rates.
